top_stocks.description:
• Micron Technology is a memory and storage semiconductor company (DRAM and NAND flash), selling into computing, mobile, cloud/data center, and embedded markets.
• Market cap/size: Large-cap U.S. semiconductor manufacturer (commonly treated as a mega/large-cap within the memory industry).
• Competitive advantage: Technology/process know-how in DRAM/NAND, strong ability to scale leading-node production, and a current industry position benefiting from memory cycle tight supply/AI-driven demand signals.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-03-18 Micron reported fiscal 2026 second-quarter results with very large year-over-year EPS and revenue growth, and issued fiscal 2026 third-quarter guidance (revenue guidance cited around the $32.75–$34.25B range and EPS around $19.15). 
• 2026-01-27 Micron announced it broke ground on a major advanced wafer fabrication facility in Singapore with an approximately $24B planned investment over 10 years and up to ~700,000 square feet of cleanroom space (production expected to begin in the second half of calendar 2028). 
• 2026-01-19 Micron signed a deal/letter of intent to acquire a Taiwan chip foundry for about $1.8B (Tongluo/PSMC), which was framed as a longer-cycle supply/capacity story (impact not expected immediately).
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=19.1 and PEG=0.04 imply the stock is priced low versus the growth rate; however, the PEG is extremely low and may reflect that “growth” is likely highly cyclical (so the valuation support may not persist through a down-cycle).
• Profitability: ROE=0.3982% and profit margin=0.4149% look weak in absolute percentage terms; this suggests earnings/margins are currently depressed or that the screener’s ROE/profit margin inputs may be normalized/after-cycle—so you should verify whether margins are improving sequentially.
• Growth: EPS growth “Next 5Y=1.0949%” appears modest; if the business is still in a memory upcycle, the near-term growth rate may be temporary, but a continued AI/storage capex environment could still help.
• Quality: Investor Score=100/100 indicates very strong screen-level quality; the main risk to watch is whether low PEG and “quality” are both being driven by a temporary cyclical peak.

top_stocks.description:
• Jabil is an electronics manufacturing services (EMS) company that designs and manufactures complex electronics for customers in sectors such as data/AI infrastructure, consumer electronics, industrial, and automotive.
• Market cap/size: Large-cap (mid-to-large sized industrial/tech services company within U.S. manufacturing/outsourced electronics).
• Competitive advantage: Broad global manufacturing footprint and engineering/operational capabilities that support high-complexity production and program scaling for major OEM/ODM customers.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-02-?? (Q1 fiscal 2026 context) Jabil reported results ahead of expectations for the first quarter of fiscal 2026, including EPS of $2.85 vs a $2.69 forecast, and discussed raised outlook/guidance.
• 2026-01-?? Jabil reported fiscal 2024/2025 results and provided guidance for fiscal 2026 periods, including core diluted EPS guidance ranges tied to revenue expectations (notably a first-quarter fiscal 2026 revenue range of $7.7B–$8.3B and core diluted EPS $2.47–$2.87).
• 2025-09-?? Coverage around FY2025 earnings indicated Jabil expects meaningful AI-related revenue contribution (a narrative catalyst commonly tied to demand recovery).
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=35.71 and PEG=0.91 suggest a more fairly valued to moderately expensive multiple for a slower-growth profile; PEG near 1 usually implies market-implied growth roughly matches expectations.
• Profitability: Profit margin=0.0248 and ROE=0.5988% are low, which is common for contract manufacturing businesses but still indicates thin spread; you’d want to confirm margins are improving with utilization.
• Growth: EPS growth “Next 5Y=0.2022%” looks extremely low, so the key question is whether the current earnings/AI-cycle tailwinds translate into sustained earnings power rather than episodic margins.
• Quality: Investor Score=85/100 is solid; given the business model, execution (cost, mix, working capital) likely matters more than long-run organic growth rates.

top_stocks.description:
• Phibro Animal Health develops and commercializes animal health products and mineral nutrition solutions for livestock and companion animals.
• Market cap/size: Small-to-mid-cap U.S. specialty healthcare/animal health company.
• Competitive advantage: Product portfolio and distribution in animal nutrition/health, with an emphasis on formulation, regulation/approval know-how, and customer relationships in feed/animal systems.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-02-04 Phibro reported fiscal 2026 second-quarter results for the quarter ended December 31, 2025, including adjusted diluted EPS of $0.87 (up 58%) and updated financial guidance for the year ending June 30, 2026.
• 2026-02-05 Coverage highlighted a major Q2 earnings beat versus consensus estimates (EPS cited around $0.87 vs ~$0.69) and associated guidance raise.
• 2026-02-06 Follow-on commentary framed the move as a re-rate catalyst after the Q2 performance and raised full-year outlook.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=23.85 and PEG=0.76 imply a reasonable value for a moderate-growth specialty business; PEG<1 suggests the market may be underpricing growth (relative to the screener’s growth assumptions), though verify normalization.
• Profitability: ROE=0.318% and profit margin=0.0629% are modest; for a smaller specialty company, this can still be acceptable, but it indicates earnings sensitivity to input costs and pricing.
• Growth: EPS growth “This Y=0.4474%” and “Next 5Y=0.2104%” are low-to-moderate, meaning the investment case likely depends on further guidance raises, product momentum, or operating leverage rather than rapid top-line expansion.
• Quality: Investor Score=85/100 is supportive; watch for execution risk (pricing, commodity/feed dynamics, and product mix) as the primary swing factor.

top_stocks.description:
• Lam Research designs and supplies semiconductor processing equipment, especially for wafer fabrication steps such as etch and deposition used in advanced logic and memory manufacturing.
• Market cap/size: Large-cap semiconductor equipment supplier.
• Competitive advantage: Deep process know-how, strong customer qualification/installed base effects, and leadership in etch/deposition tool performance that benefits from node/technology transitions.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-01-28 Lam Research reported financial results for the quarter ended December 28, 2025 (quarterly update) and provided forward-looking commentary via investor relations materials.
• 2026-02-?? / 2026-03-04 Coverage indicated Lam Research delivered results that beat expectations and raised/updated near-term guidance (one report cited Q2 earnings around $1.27/share beating by about ~9% and Q1 guidance revenue around ~$5.7B).
• 2026-03-12 Coverage noted continued strong stock performance and referenced management expectations for advanced packaging and 2026 growth considerations.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=47.82 and PEG=1.32 indicate the stock is priced at a premium relative to a growth-rate measure; that can be justified if tool demand tied to advanced nodes is durable, but it increases downside if capex slows.
• Profitability: ROIC=44.78% and profit margin=0.3022% point to very strong capital efficiency in the screener inputs; this supports the idea of a high-quality franchise if consistent through the cycle.
• Growth: EPS growth “Next 5Y=0.2502%” is moderate; with a high multiple, investors need steady execution and continued customer capex.
• Quality: Investor Score=85/100 aligns with a high-quality equipment profile; key risks are cyclicality (WFE/semicapex) and any demand digestion after strong quarters.

top_stocks.description:
• KLA is a semiconductor process control and inspection equipment company, providing metrology and defect inspection tools that help chipmakers achieve yields on advanced nodes.
• Market cap/size: Large-cap semiconductor equipment/technology supplier.
• Competitive advantage: Installed base and workflow/software/process control expertise, benefiting from the increasing need for inspection/metrology as feature sizes shrink and defect sensitivity rises.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-01-29 KLA reported fiscal 2026 second-quarter results for the quarter ended December 31, 2025, including GAAP net income of about $1.15B, GAAP EPS of $8.68, and revenues of about $3.30B, along with guidance for the following quarter.
• 2026-01-29 Earnings-call coverage emphasized strong profitability, record free cash flow, and that KLA is effectively constrained/sold-out across many products in the first half of 2026 due to supply chain bottlenecks and long-lead components.
• 2026-02-?? Guidance/coverage further discussed constraints and the outlook for demand visibility and backlog impacting 2026 shipments.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=43.91 and PEG=1.75 indicate a premium valuation versus the screener’s growth-rate assumption; this is reasonable only if inspection/metrology demand remains structurally strong and growth/upgrades accelerate.
• Profitability: ROIC=39.55% and profit margin=0.3578% are high in the screener inputs, supporting the idea of strong business quality and value capture.
• Growth: EPS growth “Next 5Y=0.1801%” is not high; with a high P/E/PEG, the market is likely pricing in more than just that baseline (e.g., mix improvements and structural demand).
• Quality: Investor Score=80/100 is solid; the main bull case is sustained process-control intensity and backlog conversion, while the key risk is that premium valuation could compress if WFE spending or customer utilization softens.
